Okay, let me think through this... 

First, x is set to 10. Then it checks if x > 5 (which is true since 10 > 5), so it should print 'A'. The elif and else parts don't matter because the first condition was already true. 

But wait... I'm not totally sure about how elif works. Does it check all conditions or stop after the first true one? I think it stops after the first match, so only 'A' prints. 

My answer is: A
